title: BAAN Changes the Rules of the ERP Game with Dynamic Enterprise Modeling
author: Aberdeen Group
source_file: 1996 BAAN Changes the Rules of the ERP Game apr.pdf
abstract: Aberdeen Group evaluates Baan Company's April 1996 release of BAAN IV, an enterprise resource planning suite featuring Dynamic Enterprise Modeling (DEM) and Orgware technology. The report argues that BAAN IV's process-oriented, dynamically configurable architecture fundamentally advances ERP software beyond the static, parameter-heavy systems of competitors, giving Baan at least a 12-month competitive lead. Aberdeen concludes that Baan should be on every serious ERP evaluation shortlist.
importance_rationale: Aberdeen's endorsement of BAAN IV as a category-redefining product coincided with Baan's peak market momentum (Boeing win, 1995 IPO, 91% revenue growth); the study introduced the 'Dynamic-ERP vs. Static-ERP' framework that shaped evaluations across the industry through the late 1990s.
relevance_rationale: The Dynamic Enterprise Modeling concept foreshadowed model-driven ERP configuration approaches still relevant today; specific product claims and competitive landscape are dated given Baan's subsequent acquisition by Invensys and eventual absorption into Infor LN.
prescience_rationale: Aberdeen predicted Baan's 12-month competitive lead and rising market stature; Baan's 1998 revenue manipulation scandal, loss of customers to SAP, and 2000 sale to Invensys for $700M (against a $7B peak cap) proved these claims premature and the competitive advantage was short-lived.
